@aibast-agents-library/account_intellige.text: A thorough 456-line agent that delivers genuine 360-degree account briefings — the combination of stakeholder mapping, competitive analysis, and deal risk assessment in a single file is impressive. The five-tag coverage from competitive-intel to stakeholder-mapping reflects real depth, not just surface-level CRM mirroring.

At 457 lines this orchestrator coordinates multi-agent pipelines for account intelligence, which is architecturally significant — it's the glue that makes the B2B sales suite work as a cohesive system. The orchestration and pipeline tags are well-earned; this is a genuine coordination layer, not just a wrapper.

@aibast-agents-library/action_prioritiza.text: A focused 398-line agent that tackles the daily planning and resource allocation problem for sales teams. The action-prioritization and planning tags accurately describe what it does — turning pipeline noise into ranked to-do lists. Solid utility agent that every sales org needs.

@aibast-agents-library/competitive_intel.text: Battlecard generation and win/loss pattern analysis in 441 lines — this agent addresses one of the hardest problems in enterprise sales: turning competitive noise into actionable positioning. The competitive-intelligence and battlecards tags signal real tactical value for deal teams.

@aibast-agents-library/deal_tracking.text: A well-scoped 402-line agent covering pipeline movement, stage velocity, and forecast accuracy. The deal-tracking and forecasting tags reflect its dual nature as both an operational tracker and a predictive tool. Clean, focused, and essential for any pipeline-driven sales org.

@aibast-agents-library/meeting_prep.text: At 521 lines and 25.1 KB, this is one of the meatier B2B agents — and for good reason. Pre-meeting briefs, talking points, objection prep, and follow-up templates cover the full meeting lifecycle. The objection-handling tag is a standout; few agents tackle that preparation layer.

@aibast-agents-library/account_messaging.text: A 429-line agent generating personalized outreach and campaign sequences for enterprise accounts. The messaging and email-sequences tags accurately describe its scope — this is the content engine for account-based campaigns. Practical and immediately deployable.

@aibast-agents-library/account_risk_asse.text: At 495 lines, this agent goes beyond simple risk flags — it assesses churn probability and financial health with mitigation recommendations. The risk-assessment and churn-prediction tags reflect genuine analytical depth. A critical early-warning system for account teams.

@aibast-agents-library/stakeholder_intel.text: Org chart mapping, buying committee analysis, and relationship strength scoring in 440 lines — this agent tackles the political layer of enterprise sales that most tools ignore. At 23.4 KB, it packs significant logic into stakeholder-mapping and buying-committee analysis.

@aibast-agents-library/activity_gap.text: A lean 393-line agent that identifies missing sales activities per deal stage and analyzes gap impact. The activity-gap and deal-progression tags capture its niche perfectly — it's the agent that tells you what you forgot to do before the deal stalls. Smart, focused scope.

@aibast-agents-library/deal_competitor_i.text: Compact at 334 lines, this agent delivers competitive snapshots and threat scoring at the individual deal level. The competitive-intelligence tag is shared with its portfolio-level sibling, but the per-deal focus with counter-strategies makes it tactically distinct and immediately useful.

@aibast-agents-library/deal_health_score.text: A focused 378-line agent that calculates deal health scores with trend analysis and benchmark comparison. The scoring and pipeline tags reflect its quantitative approach — this is the agent that turns gut feelings about deal quality into numbers. Clean and purposeful.

@aibast-agents-library/deal_progression.text: The largest deal-progression agent at 643 lines and 41.6 KB — this is a comprehensive pipeline management engine with stalled-deal detection, action plan generation, and pipeline acceleration. The breadth from health analysis to intervention planning in a single file is exceptional engineering.

@aibast-agents-library/deal_risk_assessm.text: A solid 424-line agent offering multi-factor risk scoring, risk matrices, and mitigation planning for pipeline deals. The risk-assessment and deal-progression tags position it as the quantitative complement to the account-level risk agent. Well-structured and analytically sound.

@aibast-agents-library/next_best_action.text: At 430 lines, this agent recommends prioritized actions, sequences tasks, and forecasts impact — the holy grail of sales productivity. The next-best-action tag is the star here; combining action recommendation with rep assignment optimization shows real workflow understanding.

@aibast-agents-library/pipeline_velocity.text: A 380-line agent providing velocity dashboards, stage analysis, and bottleneck detection. The pipeline-velocity and analytics tags are precisely right — this is the speedometer for your sales pipeline. The acceleration planning feature elevates it beyond pure reporting.

@aibast-agents-library/revenue_forecast.text: A focused 348-line forecasting agent with scenario analysis and commit vs best-case modeling. The revenue-forecast and analytics tags describe exactly what it delivers — multiple forecast views plus accuracy tracking. Lean, purposeful, and essential for sales leadership.

@aibast-agents-library/deal_stakeholder_.text: A 398-line agent covering engagement scoring, relationship mapping, and sentiment analysis for deal stakeholders. The stakeholder-engagement and relationships tags reflect its people-centric approach to deal management. A nice complement to the broader stakeholder intelligence agent.

@aibast-agents-library/stalled_deal_dete.text: At 453 lines, this agent goes beyond simple detection — it classifies root causes, generates intervention plans, and provides stall prevention guidance. The stalled-deals tag understates its scope; this is really a deal recovery system. The root cause classification is a standout feature.

@aibast-agents-library/win_probability.text: A 414-line agent calculating win probabilities with contributing factor analysis and trend tracking. The win-probability and forecasting tags capture its predictive nature well. The deal comparison feature adds practical value for prioritizing where to invest selling time.

@aibast-agents-library/proposal_generati.text: At 673 lines and 34.6 KB, this is a heavyweight agent with RFP analysis, personalized content generation, pricing optimization, and competitive positioning — essentially a full proposal factory. Six tags across proposal, rfp, pricing, and competitive-positioning reflect genuine breadth.

@aibast-agents-library/sales_qualificati.text: At 570 lines and a substantial 47.4 KB, this agent packs serious logic into ICP scoring, BANT analysis, outreach generation, AE routing, and SLA tracking. The six tags from lead-qualification to lead-routing reflect a complete inbound lead processing pipeline in a single file.

@aibast-agents-library/win_loss_analysis.text: The crown jewel of the B2B suite at 921 lines and 89.7 KB — by far the largest agent in this batch. AI-powered pattern recognition, competitive insights, revenue recovery modeling, and board-ready presentations make this a genuine strategic analysis engine. Exceptional depth and ambition.

@aibast-agents-library/cart_abandonment_.text: A compact 289-line agent tackling cart abandonment with recovery campaigns, incentive optimization, and conversion tracking. The ecommerce and conversion tags position it squarely in the B2C revenue recovery space. Lean and focused on the single highest-impact e-commerce problem.

@aibast-agents-library/customer_360_spee.text: A 289-line agent that unifies customer profiles with interaction history, sentiment analysis, and next-best-action recommendations. The combination of customer-360 and speech tags is unique — bridging profile data with conversational intelligence. The omnichannel tag hints at cross-channel unification.

@aibast-agents-library/customer_loyalty_.text: A focused 292-line loyalty program manager with dashboards, points tracking, reward recommendations, and tier analytics. The loyalty and rewards tags are straightforward, but the tier analytics feature adds strategic depth beyond simple points ledgering. Solid B2C retention tool.

@aibast-agents-library/omnichannel_engag.text: At 273 lines, this is the leanest agent in the B2C suite — but it covers channel performance, journey mapping, optimization, and campaign attribution. The omnichannel and attribution tags signal its cross-channel analytical focus. A compact but well-rounded engagement analytics tool.

@aibast-agents-library/personalized_shop.text: A 266-line agent offering product recommendations, style profiling, inventory checks, and outfit building. The personalization and style tags capture its consumer-facing nature — this is the closest thing to a virtual personal shopper in the registry. Fun and practical.

@aibast-agents-library/returns_exchange.text: A 331-line agent managing the full returns lifecycle — initiation, eligibility checking, exchange options, and refund tracking. The returns and customer-service tags reflect its operational focus. At 15.3 KB, it packs enough logic to handle real-world return policy complexity.

@aibast-agents-library/sales_chat.text: At 360 lines, this conversational sales agent handles product inquiries, availability checks, promotion lookups, and order assistance. The chat and promotion tags highlight its interactive nature — this is the front-line agent customers actually talk to. The broadest B2C customer touchpoint.

@aibast-agents-library/asset_maintenance.text: A 328-line predictive maintenance agent for energy infrastructure with asset health monitoring, budget projections, and work order planning. The predictive tag is key — this goes beyond reactive maintenance into forecasting, which is where the real operational savings live.

@aibast-agents-library/emission_tracking.text: A 328-line GHG emissions monitoring agent covering regulatory compliance, reduction plans, and carbon offset analysis. The sustainability and ghg tags reflect genuine environmental focus — this is a compliance necessity that's only growing in importance. Timely and well-scoped.

@aibast-agents-library/field_service_dis.text: At 369 lines, this agent manages field service dispatch with route optimization, technician assignment, and emergency response for energy infrastructure. The dispatch and emergency tags highlight its operational urgency — when a transformer fails at 2 AM, this is the agent you need.

@aibast-agents-library/permit_license_ma.text: A 341-line agent tracking permits and licenses across energy facilities with renewal calendars and compliance gap identification. The permits and renewals tags capture a critical but often overlooked operational need — missing a permit renewal can shut down an entire facility.

@aibast-agents-library/energy_regulatory.text: A 326-line agent managing regulatory report status, data validation, and submission tracking for EPA, FERC, and state filings. The regulatory and audit tags reflect its compliance-focused design. The multi-authority coverage across EPA and FERC is a practical strength.

@aibast-agents-library/acquisition_suppo.text: A 398-line agent supporting the federal acquisition lifecycle with FAR/DFAR compliance, vendor evaluation, and procurement timeline management. The FAR and DFAR tags signal deep domain knowledge — navigating federal acquisition regulations is notoriously complex, and this agent tackles it head-on.

@aibast-agents-library/federal_grants_ov.text: A 328-line agent for federal grants monitoring with compliance tracking, reporting status, and audit preparation. The grants and oversight tags accurately describe its watchdog role. At 15.1 KB, it packs enough logic for real grants management workflows.

@aibast-agents-library/mission_reporting.text: A compact 286-line agent generating mission summaries, KPI dashboards, and stakeholder briefs for federal programs. The mission and KPI tags reflect its focus on translating program outcomes into leadership-ready reporting. Lean and purpose-built for federal program offices.

@aibast-agents-library/regulatory_compli.text: A 290-line agent covering FISMA, FedRAMP, and NIST gap analysis with remediation planning and audit readiness. The three-framework coverage (FISMA, FedRAMP, NIST) in under 300 lines is impressive engineering — these are the compliance frameworks that define federal IT security.

@aibast-agents-library/workforce_clearan.text: A 338-line agent tracking federal workforce clearances, onboarding checklists, and access provisioning. The clearance and background-check tags highlight a uniquely federal need — security clearance management is a workflow that simply doesn't exist in the private sector.

@aibast-agents-library/claims_processing.text: A 295-line insurance claims processing agent with intake, adjudication review, fraud detection, and settlement recommendations. The claims and adjudication tags reflect its end-to-end scope across the claims lifecycle. Compact but covers the critical path from filing to settlement.

@aibast-agents-library/fs_customer_onboa.text: A 317-line agent handling financial services customer onboarding with KYC verification, account setup, and document checklists. The KYC and compliance tags are the standout — know-your-customer verification is the regulatory gate that every financial institution must clear.

@aibast-agents-library/customer_sentimen.text: A 327-line agent combining customer sentiment analysis with churn prediction and retention action planning. The NPS and churn tags reflect its dual analytical-and-actionable nature — it doesn't just measure satisfaction, it prescribes retention interventions. Strong analytical depth.

@aibast-agents-library/financial_advisor.text: A 292-line copilot for financial advisors with client reviews, portfolio summaries, and compliance checks. The advisor and portfolio tags position it as an augmentation tool rather than a replacement — it helps advisors do their job better, which is the right approach for regulated industries.

@aibast-agents-library/fraud_detection_a.text: A compact 272-line agent for financial fraud detection with alert triage, transaction analysis, and investigation case management. The fraud and detection tags undersell its scope — the combination of real-time alerting with investigation workflow support makes this a genuine security operations tool.

@aibast-agents-library/loan_origination_.text: A 327-line agent supporting loan origination with application review, credit analysis, and decision recommendations. The loan and underwriting tags reflect its position in the lending pipeline. Document verification plus credit analysis in a single agent covers the core origination workflow.

@aibast-agents-library/portfolio_rebalan.text: A thorough 306-line financial services agent that covers the full rebalancing lifecycle — drift analysis, trade recommendations, tax impact assessment, and execution planning. The inclusion of tax-aware logic elevates this beyond a simple allocation tool. Strong entry for any wealth-tech stack.

@aibast-agents-library/fs_regulatory_com.text: Tackles the notoriously complex regulatory landscape across SOX, Dodd-Frank, and BSA/AML in a focused 268 lines. Examiner preparation and remediation planning are standout features that show real domain understanding. A must-have for compliance-heavy financial organizations.

@aibast-agents-library/underwriting_supp.text: At 329 lines this is one of the meatier financial services agents, covering risk evaluation, pricing recommendations, guideline compliance, and exception review. The breadth of underwriting concerns addressed in a single file is impressive. Well-suited for insurance carriers looking to augment human underwriters.

@aibast-agents-library/wealth_insights_g.text: Delivers a solid toolkit for wealth management teams in 280 lines — market briefs, client analytics, opportunity alerts, and performance attribution. The analytics and performance attribution tags suggest meaningful data processing capabilities. A clean, focused agent for advisory practices.

@aibast-agents-library/ai_customer_assis.text: One of the larger general-purpose agents at 369 lines and 18.2 KB, packing customer inquiries, knowledge search, escalation routing, and satisfaction surveys into a single cohesive flow. The escalation logic paired with CSAT surveys shows end-to-end service design thinking. Versatile across industries.

@aibast-agents-library/general_ask_hr.text: The largest general-category agent in this batch at 402 lines, covering policy lookups, benefits inquiries, leave requests, and employee directory searches. The breadth of HR self-service operations packed into one file is remarkable. An excellent foundation for any employee-facing HR chatbot.

@aibast-agents-library/bulk_crm_data_gen.text: A practical 264-line utility agent that generates synthetic CRM contacts, accounts, and opportunities for testing and demos. Not glamorous, but invaluable for development teams needing realistic test data without touching production. The data-migration tag hints at additional portability features.

@aibast-agents-library/dynamics_365_conn.text: A robust 325-line connector covering entity queries, record creation, bulk import, and schema inspection for D365. The schema inspection feature is a thoughtful addition that enables dynamic discovery rather than hard-coded mappings. Essential plumbing for any Microsoft-stack CRM deployment.

@aibast-agents-library/one_click_crm_int.text: Streamlines CRM data entry in 293 lines with form generation, validation, duplicate detection, and import preview. The duplicate detection capability alone justifies adoption — dirty data is the #1 CRM problem. Clean and focused on reducing data-entry friction.

@aibast-agents-library/cross_selling.text: An analytically rich 300-line agent that identifies cross-sell opportunities through product affinity analysis, customer ownership mapping, and revenue impact projections. The combination of affinity modeling with revenue projections makes recommendations actionable, not just informational. A revenue-growth multiplier.

@aibast-agents-library/customer_360.text: Unifies CRM, support, and billing data into a single customer view across 317 lines, complete with health scores and next-best-action recommendations. The multi-source data fusion approach is architecturally sound, and next-best-action pushes this from passive reporting to proactive engagement. A strategic asset.

@aibast-agents-library/email_drafting.text: A well-scoped 284-line agent covering outreach, follow-ups, proposals, and template management with personalization. The template management system keeps email quality consistent while personalization prevents robotic-sounding outreach. Solid productivity booster for sales and marketing teams.

@aibast-agents-library/find_accurate_mod.text: An interesting 285-line agent that tackles AI/ML model selection by comparing accuracy benchmarks, deployment readiness, and cost analysis. Filling the meta-AI niche of helping teams choose the right model is clever positioning. Useful for organizations navigating the growing model marketplace.

@aibast-agents-library/identify_discount.text: A focused 296-line agent handling discount eligibility checks, savings calculations, and approval workflows. The approval workflow management adds governance to what could otherwise be a free-for-all discounting process. Practical for sales teams needing pricing guardrails.

@aibast-agents-library/it_ticket_managem.text: A compact 258-line agent delivering dashboards, priority assignment, SLA tracking, and resolution reporting for IT operations. Covers the essential ITSM lifecycle efficiently without bloat. The SLA tracking capability makes it particularly valuable for teams with contractual service commitments.

@aibast-agents-library/procurement_agent.text: Handles the core procurement lifecycle — purchase requests, vendor comparison, approval routing, and spend analysis — in a lean 250 lines. The spend analysis feature enables strategic procurement insights beyond transactional processing. A good fit for mid-market procurement teams.

@aibast-agents-library/procurement_suppo.text: A complementary procurement tool at 240 lines focusing on requisition tracking, contract lookups, supplier performance, and budget checks. Leaner than its sibling procurement_agent, with a focus on operational support rather than strategic analysis. Works well as a day-to-day procurement assistant.

@aibast-agents-library/product_reference.text: A clean 267-line product catalog agent with feature comparison, pricing tiers, and compatibility checking. The compatibility checking feature is a smart inclusion that prevents costly misconfigurations. Simple, effective, and immediately useful for sales engineering teams.

@aibast-agents-library/sales_coach.text: Packs call reviews, skill assessments, coaching plans, and performance dashboards into 283 lines of sales enablement. The structured coaching plan generation transforms subjective feedback into actionable development paths. A modern approach to sales team development.

@aibast-agents-library/sales_simulation.text: A creative 306-line training agent featuring buyer personas, objection practice, and performance scoring. Turning sales training into interactive simulation with scoring is a genuinely innovative approach. The persona-based objection practice makes rehearsal realistic rather than theoretical.

@aibast-agents-library/speech_to_crm.text: Bridges the voice-to-data gap in 265 lines with call transcription, entity extraction, CRM field mapping, and update previews. The entity extraction and field mapping pipeline shows sophisticated NLP design thinking. A high-value automation that eliminates tedious post-call data entry.

@aibast-agents-library/triage_bot.text: A focused 253-line agent handling inquiry classification, routing, priority assessment, and handoff summaries. The handoff summary generation ensures context isn't lost during escalation — a frequently overlooked detail. Efficient and well-scoped for front-line support operations.

@aibast-agents-library/voice_to_crm_d365.text: Extends the voice-to-CRM pattern specifically for Dynamics 365 in 253 lines, adding voice capture, entity extraction, record updates, and sync tracking. The D365-specific sync tracking ensures data consistency across the voice-to-record pipeline. A targeted integration that fills a real gap in the Microsoft ecosystem.

@aibast-agents-library/voice_to_crm_emai.text: A practical 258-line agent that generates meeting recaps, extracts action items, drafts follow-up emails, and manages distribution lists. The action-item extraction into follow-up emails automates one of the most commonly dropped balls in business communication. Reliable meeting-to-action pipeline.

@aibast-agents-library/sharepoint_docume.text: A utility agent at 243 lines delivering SharePoint document search, URL extraction, metadata enrichment, and link validation. The link validation feature prevents the all-too-common broken SharePoint link problem. A quiet but essential tool for Microsoft 365 environments.

@aibast-agents-library/voice_to_crm_serv.text: Connects voice input to ServiceNow workflows in 264 lines — incident creation, knowledge search, assignment routing, and status updates. Integrating voice-driven incident creation with knowledge base search creates a natural self-service pathway. Solid ITSM integration for ServiceNow shops.

@aibast-agents-library/care_gap_closure.text: A clinically sophisticated 324-line agent that analyzes HEDIS quality measure gaps, prioritizes patient outreach, manages campaigns, and provides compliance dashboards. The HEDIS-specific quality measure focus shows deep healthcare domain expertise. Critical for value-based care organizations chasing quality bonuses.

@aibast-agents-library/clinical_notes_su.text: At 354 lines, this healthcare agent handles encounter summarization, medication reviews, problem list generation, and referral summaries. The medication review capability alongside clinical summarization addresses a real patient safety concern. A high-value clinical workflow accelerator.

@aibast-agents-library/patient_intake.text: A comprehensive 369-line agent managing intake forms, insurance verification, appointment scheduling, and pre-visit summary prep. Covering the entire pre-visit workflow from registration through insurance verification reduces front-desk bottlenecks significantly. Well-designed for ambulatory care settings.

@aibast-agents-library/prior_authorizati.text: Tackles one of healthcare's biggest pain points in 364 lines — PA requests, clinical criteria checks, status tracking, and appeal preparation. The appeal preparation feature is a standout that addresses the 30%+ initial denial rate most providers face. Potentially the highest-ROI agent in the healthcare vertical.

@aibast-agents-library/staff_credentiali.text: A focused 310-line agent for credential tracking, expiration alerts, verification audits, and onboarding checklists. DEA and license tracking with automated expiration alerts prevents costly compliance lapses. Essential back-office automation for healthcare organizations of any size.

@aibast-agents-library/ask_hr.text: The most fully-featured HR agent in the batch at 390 lines, covering time-off requests, benefits inquiries, parental leave, and policy lookups. The parental leave specialization alongside general benefits handling shows attention to the queries employees actually struggle with. A polished employee self-service experience.

@aibast-agents-library/it_helpdesk.text: The largest agent in this batch at 425 lines, delivering automated troubleshooting, knowledge retrieval, remote remediation, and ticket management. The remote remediation capability pushes this beyond typical chatbot territory into genuine IT operations automation. A flagship-quality IT support agent.

@aibast-agents-library/inventory_rebalan.text: A supply-chain powerhouse at 353 lines that optimizes multi-warehouse distribution by analyzing stock levels against demand forecasts and generating cost-effective transfer plans. The demand-forecast integration makes rebalancing proactive rather than reactive. Strong manufacturing vertical entry.

@aibast-agents-library/maintenance_sched.text: Generates predictive maintenance schedules from equipment telemetry and failure models in 367 lines, with technician assignment optimization. The IoT telemetry integration combined with failure modeling represents sophisticated predictive analytics. A high-value agent for reducing unplanned downtime.

@aibast-agents-library/order_status_comm.text: A 321-line agent tracking manufacturing orders through fulfillment with proactive delay notifications and customer status updates. The proactive delay notification capability turns a reactive process into a trust-building customer experience. Clean execution of a frequently-needed manufacturing communication flow.

@aibast-agents-library/production_line_o.text: Analyzes OEE, identifies bottleneck stations, and generates throughput optimization plans with shift-level scheduling in 319 lines. The shift-level scheduling granularity in optimization plans makes recommendations immediately actionable on the factory floor. A data-driven approach to continuous improvement.

@aibast-agents-library/supplier_risk_mon.text: Monitors supplier risk across quality, delivery, financial, and geopolitical dimensions in 330 lines with scorecards and alternative-sourcing plans. The multi-dimensional risk scoring with geopolitical awareness reflects post-2020 supply chain realities. A strategic procurement intelligence tool.

@aibast-agents-library/client_health_sco.text: Computes client health from NPS, margins, utilization, and escalation data across 342 lines, identifying at-risk accounts and driving retention. The multi-signal health scoring approach — combining financial metrics with sentiment data — produces more reliable churn predictions. A retention-focused powerhouse.

@aibast-agents-library/contract_risk_rev.text: Scans contracts for risky clauses and evaluates compliance with internal policies in 315 lines, producing renegotiation briefs with prioritized amendments. The prioritized amendment recommendations transform legal review from a bottleneck into a decision-support process. Valuable for firms managing high contract volumes.

@aibast-agents-library/proposal_copilot.text: Generates competitive proposals with pricing models and win-theme analysis from historical data in 333 lines. The competitive positioning against known bidders using historical win/loss data is a sophisticated feature that can materially improve win rates. A strategic differentiator for professional services firms.

@aibast-agents-library/resource_utilizat.text: Tracks consultant utilization, forecasts demand, and analyzes bench costs in 335 lines with staffing recommendations. The bench cost analysis alongside demand forecasting enables proactive staffing decisions that directly impact profitability. Essential for any consulting or professional services organization.

@aibast-agents-library/time_entry_billin.text: Processes time entries against billing rules, surfaces unbilled hours, and audits for compliance in 362 lines. The unbilled-hours detection alone can recover significant revenue — most firms leak 5-10% through missed billing. A direct revenue-protection agent with strong ROI.

@aibast-agents-library/inventory_visibil.text: A substantial 370-line retail agent delivering real-time inventory dashboards, stock-out alerts, replenishment planning, and channel allocation optimization. The omni-channel allocation optimization addresses one of retail's hardest problems — getting the right inventory to the right channel. Well-architected for modern retail operations.

@aibast-agents-library/personalized_mark.text: The second-largest agent in the batch at 436 lines, covering customer segmentation, personalized campaigns, dynamic content, and marketing performance analysis. The depth of personalization — from segmentation through dynamic content to performance measurement — creates a complete marketing automation loop. A comprehensive retail marketing engine.

@aibast-agents-library/returns_complaint.text: The largest agent in this entire batch at 506 lines, automating return processing, complaint classification, resolution recommendations, and trend identification. The complaint trend analysis capability transforms individual incidents into systemic improvement insights. An exceptionally thorough customer service agent that earns its size with genuine depth.

@aibast-agents-library/store_associate_c.text: At 538 lines, this retail agent packs serious depth — product lookup, guided customer assistance scripts, daily task checklists, and performance dashboards all in one file. The copilot metaphor is well-chosen: it augments store associates rather than replacing them. A standout entry in the retail vertical.

@aibast-agents-library/supply_chain_disr.text: The most substantial retail agent at 631 lines, tackling the genuinely hard problem of supply chain resilience. Risk assessment, mitigation playbooks, and alternative supplier identification make this a comprehensive disruption response toolkit. The tags tell the story: supply-chain, disruption, risk-management, logistics — this agent takes continuity seriously.

@aibast-agents-library/building_permit_p.text: A focused 349-line government agent handling the full permit lifecycle: status tracking, review checklists, inspector assignment, and fee calculation. Covers the core workflows that every local government building department needs. Solid SLG vertical entry.

@aibast-agents-library/citizen_service_r.text: Classic 311-style municipal service management in 327 lines. Intake, routing, status tracking, and resolution summaries — the essential citizen services pipeline. The SLA tracking tag signals awareness that government responsiveness matters. Clean and purposeful.

@aibast-agents-library/foia_request_assi.text: FOIA processing is a genuinely important civic function, and this 308-line agent handles it thoughtfully: request analysis, document search, redaction review, and response preparation. The transparency tag is not just decoration — this agent serves the public right to know. One of the more meaningful government agents in the library.

@aibast-agents-library/slg_grants_manage.text: Grants management is where government funding meets accountability. At 307 lines, this agent covers grants, budget tracking, and reporting for both state and local government. Practical and necessary — every grant-funded program needs this kind of oversight tooling.

@aibast-agents-library/utility_billing_a.text: A 372-line municipal agent covering utility billing, payment assistance, and water services. The billing-plus-assistance framing is smart — it is not just about collecting payments, but helping citizens navigate their utility accounts. Good breadth across the utility, billing, and payment tags.

@aibast-agents-library/software_competit.text: Competitive intelligence done right at 421 lines. Market analysis, SaaS pricing evaluation, and threat assessment give product teams genuine strategic visibility. The competitive-intel and threat-assessment tags reflect a mature understanding of what software companies actually need to stay ahead.

@aibast-agents-library/software_customer.text: Customer onboarding is where SaaS churn battles are won or lost. This 379-line agent tracks adoption milestones and customer success metrics — the critical first-90-days workflow. Clean integration of onboarding, adoption, and milestones into a single-file agent.

@aibast-agents-library/license_renewal_e.text: Revenue retention in 351 lines. License renewal tracking, expansion opportunity identification, and churn prevention — the three pillars of SaaS revenue ops. The renewal-plus-expansion framing shows strategic thinking beyond simple license management.

@aibast-agents-library/product_feedback_.text: This 363-line agent bridges the gap between raw user feedback and actionable product roadmap decisions. Feature request aggregation, NPS sentiment analysis, and roadmap alignment make it a genuine product management tool. The feedback-to-roadmap pipeline is exactly what PM teams need.

@aibast-agents-library/support_ticket_re.text: A well-structured 324-line support agent covering triage, SLA management, knowledge base integration, and escalation paths. Every software product needs this workflow — ticket intake to resolution with proper escalation gates. Reliable and complete.

@borg/borg_agent.text: The original Borg — 102 lines of concentrated purpose. Analysis, GitHub integration, and web assimilation in a compact core agent. This is the seed from which the entire @borg namespace grew. Small but iconic, and every line earns its place.

@borg/cardsmith_agent.text: CardSmith is a fan favorite at just 104 lines — proof that a brilliant concept needs no bloat. MTG-style trading card generation with sneakernet distribution is the kind of creative, joyful engineering that makes this ecosystem special. Howard Hoy's signature contribution to the registry.

@borg/markdown_to_slides_agent.text: A clean 236-line converter turning markdown into presentation decks. Part of the @borg productivity pipeline — write in markdown, present in slides. The deck and pipeline tags place it correctly as a document-transformation utility. Straightforward and effective.

@borg/prompt_to_video_agent.text: At 792 lines, this agent turns text prompts into rendered MP4 video via Remotion — a genuinely ambitious capability. Scene composition, rendering pipeline, and video output in a single file. The prompt-to-video workflow pushes the boundaries of what a single-file agent can accomplish.

@borg/recon_agent.text: A flagship security reconnaissance agent at 742 lines. URL scanning, SSL analysis, header inspection, tech-stack fingerprinting, and API discovery — this is a serious security tool, not a toy. The breadth of the recon, security, scanner, headers, and ssl tags reflects genuine depth. One of the most capable agents in the entire registry.

@borg/training_quest_agent.text: Gamified onboarding in 689 lines — interactive HTML quest-based training that makes learning engaging rather than tedious. The gamification approach to employee onboarding is creative and effective. Tags like quest, interactive, and gamification signal that this is not just another training checklist.

@borg/tuftelove_agent.text: A 512-line design oversight agent inspired by Edward Tufte's principles — UI review, accessibility checks, data visualization critique, and UX assessment. Naming it after Tufte sets a high bar, and the agent delivers with thoughtful coverage across design, accessibility, and data-viz. A unique and cultured addition to the registry.

@borg/vaultmind_agent.text: At 3260 lines with 28 documented actions, VaultMind is the largest and most ambitious single-file agent in the entire registry. Obsidian vault management, 30-60-90 day onboarding plans, wiki automation, knowledge base operations, scheduling, and monitoring — all in one file. This is an extraordinary engineering achievement that pushes the single-file paradigm to its absolute limit. A masterwork.

@discreetRappers/agent_generator_agent.text: Meta-engineering at its finest: a 1257-line agent that generates other agents. Scaffolding, template rendering, and code generation give the RAPP ecosystem its self-replicating capability. The pipeline category is exactly right — this is core infrastructure for agent creation.

@discreetRappers/agent_transpiler_agent.text: Cross-platform agent transpilation in 1035 lines — converting RAPP agents to M365 and Copilot Studio formats. The transpiler concept is architecturally significant: write once in RAPP, deploy to Microsoft's ecosystem. A key piece of the multi-platform story.

@discreetRappers/architecture_diagram_ag.text: Nearly 996 lines of diagramming power — generating architecture visualizations with Mermaid syntax. System documentation is often neglected; this agent makes it effortless. The productivity category undersells it — this is a genuine documentation accelerator.

@discreetRappers/copilot_studio_transpil.text: The definitive RAPP-to-Copilot-Studio bridge at 2154 lines — the largest transpiler in the registry. Native no-code output generation means RAPP agents can land directly in Microsoft's low-code ecosystem. This is strategically critical infrastructure and a remarkable single-file achievement.

@discreetRappers/demo_script_generator_a.text: At 1201 lines, this agent generates structured demo scripts in JSON format — scripted, repeatable product demonstrations. In a world where demos make or break deals, automating script creation is genuinely valuable. The generator-plus-scripted approach ensures consistency across sales teams.

@discreetRappers/dynamics_crud_agent.text: A focused 418-line integration agent providing CRUD operations against Dynamics 365 CRM. Clean Microsoft stack integration — no frills, just reliable data operations against a major enterprise platform. The integrations category and dynamics-365 tag place it precisely.

@discreetRappers/email_drafting_agent.text: Compact at 149 lines, this agent drafts emails via Power Automate integration. Small but practical — email automation is one of the most commonly requested business workflows. The power-automate tag signals proper Microsoft ecosystem integration rather than reinventing the wheel.

@discreetRappers/powerpoint_generator_ag.text: A substantial 1549-line agent generating PowerPoint presentations with template support. In the Microsoft enterprise world, PowerPoint is the lingua franca of business communication. Template-driven generation ensures brand consistency while automating the most tedious part of deck creation.

@discreetRappers/project_tracker_agent.text: Project management distilled into 894 lines. Tracking, status management, and pipeline oversight — the essential PM toolkit. The pipeline category reflects its role as orchestration infrastructure, not just a simple task list. Substantial and well-scoped.

@discreetRappers/rapp_pipeline_agent.text: The crown jewel of the pipeline category at 2984 lines. Transcript-to-agent code generation with quality gates — this is the RAPP platform's core value proposition made real. From raw conversation to deployable agent in a single pipeline run. The code-gen and quality-gates tags describe a mature, production-grade system.

@discreetRappers/sales_assistant_agent.text: A 631-line sales integration agent with natural-language CRM interaction. Bridging conversational AI with sales data is a high-value use case. The natural-language tag distinguishes this from rigid CRUD-style CRM tools — it meets salespeople where they work.

@discreetRappers/scripted_demo_agent.text: The interactive counterpart to the demo script generator at 1301 lines. Scripted, step-by-step product demos with interactive elements for live sales situations. The sales tag alongside interactive and scripted reflects a demo agent built for real customer-facing scenarios.

@discreetRappers/sharepoint_contract_ana.text: A powerhouse at 2468 lines — SharePoint document analysis with contract parsing, legal clause extraction, and compliance review. This is enterprise-grade document intelligence in a single file. The sharepoint, contracts, and legal tags represent a genuinely complex integration surface, handled impressively.

@kody/agent_workbench.text: The developer workshop at 723 lines — scaffolding, validation, testing, and publishing tools for agent authors. Every healthy ecosystem needs great developer tooling, and the Workbench delivers. The devtools category is well-earned; this is essential infrastructure for agent contributors.

@kody/context_memory_agent.text: Context and recall in a compact 183-line core agent. Memory is what separates a stateless tool from an intelligent assistant, and this agent provides the recall mechanism that other agents depend on. Small but foundational to the ecosystem's memory architecture.

@kody/deal_desk_agent.text: B2B sales intelligence in 245 lines — account intelligence, competitive analysis, and pipeline management packed into a focused deal-desk workflow. The combination of deck generation with deal and competitive tags shows this agent understands that sales is about preparation, not just tracking.

@kody/github_agent_library_agent.text: The registry client itself at 957 lines — package discovery, installation, and dependency resolution for the RAPP ecosystem. This is the npm-install of the agent world. The package-manager and discovery tags reflect its role as the primary interface between developers and the agent registry.

@kody/manage_memory_agent.text: Persistent memory management in 255 lines. Where context_memory_agent handles recall, this agent handles the storage and persistence layer. The core category placement is correct — without persistent memory, agents forget everything between sessions.

@kody/rappter_engine_agent.text: The meta-framework at 479 lines — a rules-as-data content engine that serves as the base class for every engine in the ecosystem (Zoo, Economy, Academy, Rappterpedia). Subclass it, define rules, override tick(). The heartbeat tag signals its role as a continuously-running automation backbone. Architecturally foundational and elegantly designed.

@kody/rappterbook_agent.text: A 160-line social network integration agent implementing federation and read-only data sloshing for the Rappterbook platform. The data-sloshing concept — letting data flow between federated nodes — is an interesting architectural pattern. Compact and purpose-built.

@kody/rappterpedia_agent.text: The community wiki and forum engine at 540 lines, powered by the rappter_engine pattern. Wiki articles, forum threads, and content generation in one agent. The rappterpedia and engine tags reflect its dual nature: both a content platform and a demonstration of the engine framework's power.

@kody/rar_remote_agent.text: The full-featured registry client at 840 lines — installation, discovery, voting, and community interaction with the RAPP Agent Registry. More than just a package manager, the voting and community tags reveal its role as the social layer of the ecosystem. This is how agents and developers participate in the registry.

@kody/recon_deck_agent.text: Multi-agent intelligence briefing in 175 compact lines. Coordinates Borg recon data, Rappterbook feeds, and Hacker News signals into a unified briefing deck. The multi-agent tag is key — this is an orchestrator, not a solo performer, and it demonstrates the power of agent composition.

@kody-w/hello_world_agent.text: The canonical starter template at just 39 lines — the smallest possible RAPP agent that actually works. Every agent author's first step begins here. Do not let the simplicity fool you: this template establishes the manifest pattern, BasicAgent inheritance, and perform() contract that the entire ecosystem follows.

@rapp/basic_agent.text: Twenty-nine lines that hold up the entire ecosystem. BasicAgent is the abstract base class that every single RAPP agent inherits from — the foundation stone. Its deliberate minimalism is its genius: just enough structure to enforce the contract, nothing more. Without this file, there is no registry. The most important 29 lines in RAPP.

@wildhaven/ceo_agent.text: A 325-line digital twin of WildHaven's CEO — strategy, leadership, and stewardship encoded as an agent. The digital-twin concept applied to executive decision-making is both ambitious and thought-provoking. The stewardship tag reflects leadership as service rather than just authority.
